title: Bacterial transformation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"em7lc","text":"Standard protocol to transform bacteria with a plasmid using chemically competent E. coli and antibiotic resistance.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"texX6","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"lMHKz","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"tpyoJ","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":1,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":119776,"guid":"4198CDDE341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"MACH1 or DH5a or TOP10 or NEB stable or etc for DNA purification/miniprep","mol_weight":0,"sku":"","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":1,"name":"Contributed by users","link":"https://www.protocols.io","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/1.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/1.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}},"1":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":119777,"guid":"4198CE45341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"BL21(DE3) or BL21-Star(DE3) or Rosetta2(DE3) or etc for protein purification","mol_weight":0,"sku":"","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":1,"name":"Contributed by users","link":"https://www.protocols.io","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/1.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/1.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Thaw cells on ice. All steps are in a microcentrifuge tube. 00:10:00
Add ~10-500 ng DNA (typically just use 1 uL) to each tube of cells.
Heat shock at 42 degrees for one minute in water bath. 00:01:00, heat shock Undetermined
Recover for two minutes on ice. 00:02:00
Add 180 μL LB or SOC media, mix. undetermined, LB or SOC media

Grow at 37 degrees for one hour with shaking. Warm the plates during this step. 01:00:00, growth Undetermined If a faster transformation is desired the growth step can be skipped entirely for ampicillin resistant plasmids, or shortened to ~15 minutes for kanamycin resistant plasmids. Growth may increase the number of colonies though.
Plate 75 μL of the transformation on plate with appropriate antibiotic.
Leave at 37 degrees overnight or room temperature for two days. 16:00:00

Count the number of transformations to perform and remove the appropriate number of aliquots from the -80 to an ice bucket. Our aliquots are 100 ul each. If transformating a plasmid with repeats (such as a lentiviral plasmid), be sure to use NEB Stable or Stbl3 cells or similar.
Label microcentrifuge tubes and add 20 ul cells to each tube.
Incubate for 25 minutes on ice. Anywhere from ~5 to 25 minutes usually works. 00:25:00
creator: Stephen Floor
